title: Portable projected LifeOS build proven end to end
supporting_sources: 1.0_VISION/Notebooklm/Architecture Context Matrix_ PostgreSQL-First LifeOS Framework - Table 1.csv
store_authority_reference: STORE-001 — PostgreSQL + RuVector is the single canonical durable owner; redb is the transient forward buffer; envctl is the forward-only bridge and the projection engine; AgentDB (.rvf) is non-authoritative per-agent cognition; nu_plugin CodeDB is the parser/ingestion path.
objective: Define the smallest end-to-end proof in which one bounded LifeOS source slice completes the full round trip source -> PostgreSQL+RuVector -> envctl projection -> reproducible Nix+musl build -> running binary -> answered LifeOS UDS request, carrying byte, identity, behavior, provenance, rollback, and rebuild proofs, with every entity assigned exactly one canonical owner under STORE-001.
verification_gate: One bounded source slice completes source-to-database-to-projection-to-reproducible-build-to-running-UDS round trip with byte, identity, behavior, provenance, rollback, and rebuild proofs.
execution_gate_note: This artifact DEFINES the smallest end-to-end proof and its per-stage/per-proof acceptance criteria. It performs no live database mutation, no build, and no run: those actions are deferred to the POSTGRES execution gate (the POSTGRES-010 authority-cutover lane and its live blobstore/RuVector parity runs). Per the task allowed/blocked paths, live database mutation before the execution gate, profile installation, and production deployment are out of scope here.

bounded_source_slice.principle: The smallest LifeOS source subset that is simultaneously (a) parseable to a Nushell AST, (b) embeddable by the local model, (c) losslessly projectable to a clean directory, (d) statically musl-compilable, (e) runnable as an install-in-place folder, and (f) able to answer exactly one LifeOS UDS request. No stage in the round trip is allowed to be a no-op.
bounded_source_slice.membership_contract: An explicit include manifest enumerates every source block by module_path. Only blocks in the manifest enter the bounded PostgreSQL namespace (a dedicated schema/branch for this proof). The slice is content-addressed: each member carries a SHA-256 and a BLAKE3 dedup hash so membership is a checksummed set, not a directory glob.
exclusions: secrets/** — external-secret / provenance authority stays in the OS keyring (STORE-001 external-secret class); never ingested.

AgentDB (.rvf) per-agent cognition — non-authoritative active cognition (STORE-001 per-agent cognition class); the minimal UDS request is answered from canonical PostgreSQL state, so no agent brain is required in the slice.
Any user-space class not required to bind a socket and answer one status request (media blobs, communications, domain records) — bounded out to keep the slice minimal.

members.kind: rust AST blocks (fn main, UDS listener bootstrap)
members.owner_after_ingest: PostgreSQL — durable relational macro-state (code AST/rows, module_path metadata, causal edges)

members.kind: rust AST blocks (bind, accept, handle_request -> status response)
members.owner_after_ingest: PostgreSQL — durable relational macro-state; semantic_embedding column owned by PostgreSQL+RuVector

one_uds_request.request: lifeos.status — a single structured status/health request sent over a Unix Domain Socket (no HTTP/REST).
one_uds_request.grounding: Blueprint section 'LifeOS Unix Domain Sockets (UDS) via Yazelix (Zellij)': LifeOS communicates with the workspace via UDS (or shared redb state files) and renders swarm status without overlapping state; POSTGRES-007 requires every visible LifeOS state/command to map to canonical database identities with an explicit UDS request/response contract.
one_uds_request.expected_response: A deterministic structured record: { status: "ok", slice_id, projected_version_id, build_binary_sha256, answered_from: "postgresql-canonical" }. Every field traces to a canonical PostgreSQL identity or a recorded stage receipt — nothing is synthesized at request time.

bounded_source_slice.bound_size_rationale: One crate, three source members, one request. This is the least surface that still forces every mechanism the blueprint names: AST parse (Nushell) -> redb buffer (nu_plugin, MessagePack, BLAKE3 dedup) -> embed (all-MiniLM-L6-v2) + commit (envctl) -> PostgreSQL rows -> envctl 4-step projection -> Nix x86_64-unknown-linux-musl static build -> install-in-place run -> UDS answer.

round_trip_stages.name: source-to-database (ingestion + embedding)
round_trip_stages.blueprint_pillars: Three Pillars of Ingestion — Nushell (Parser) -> nu_plugin (redb Buffer) -> envctl (Bridge to PostgreSQL).
round_trip_stages.inputs: The bounded_source_slice include manifest (Cargo.toml, src/main.rs, src/uds.rs).
mechanism: Nushell traverses the slice and parses each member into AST blocks (functions, structs, dependencies).
nu_plugin CodeDB streams the structured blocks into the redb buffer over the MessagePack protocol at microsecond latency; redb computes a BLAKE3 hash per block as the embedding-dedup cache key.

envctl pulls the blocks from redb, runs the local all-MiniLM-L6-v2 model to produce semantic_embedding vectors (dedup-guarded by the BLAKE3 cache), and commits raw_code + semantic_embedding + module_path metadata + causal dependency edges into the bounded PostgreSQL namespace as a single production branch.

store_001_ownership.durable_relational_macro_state: PostgreSQL (single durable writer of raw_code, module_path, causal edges).
store_001_ownership.semantic_vector_state: PostgreSQL + RuVector (native extension, No-Sidecar) owns semantic_embedding.
store_001_ownership.transient_high_frequency_state: redb holds the forward buffer and BLAKE3 dedup hashes only; forward-only redb -> envctl -> PostgreSQL; no dual-write.

round_trip_stages.receipt: ingestion_receipt — { namespace_id, branch_id, source_manifest_sha256, per_member{module_path, block_shas, blake3}, envctl_commit_ids, embedding_model_pin }.
round_trip_stages.notes: Per STORE-001 open dependency, while live RuVector-in-PostgreSQL extension activation is not yet locally proven, embeddings for this proof are produced via the already-verified Bun-loaded ruvector N-API path and committed to PostgreSQL; the vector column owner remains PostgreSQL.

round_trip_stages.name: database-to-projection (envctl materialization)
round_trip_stages.blueprint_pillars: envctl Projection Engine — the four-step materialization (query -> module_path map -> concatenate -> dump).
round_trip_stages.inputs: The selected production branch/version committed in S1.
mechanism: envctl queries PostgreSQL for the selected production branch of the bounded namespace.
It uses stored module_path metadata to reconstruct the exact directory structure in memory.
It concatenates the raw_code blocks back into complete standard files (Cargo.toml, src/main.rs, src/uds.rs).
It dumps the clean, disposable directory into the Nix/Yazelix build input path.

store_001_ownership.derived_projections: PostgreSQL is the source of truth; envctl projection is read-only from PostgreSQL; the filesystem tree is derived and disposable, regenerated deterministically from rows via module_path (STORE-001 class 5).

round_trip_stages.receipt: projection_receipt — { source_branch_id, source_version_id, projected_tree_path, per_file{path, sha256, byte_len, mode}, projection_run_id }.
round_trip_stages.notes: POSTGRES-008 requires repeated projections to be byte-identical; this stage's receipt captures the per-file SHA-256 that the byte and rebuild proofs compare against.

round_trip_stages.name: projection-to-reproducible-build (Nix + musl static)
round_trip_stages.blueprint_pillars: Portable Release Strategy — Nix deterministic compilation against x86_64-unknown-linux-musl; gitkb/meta isolates pure Rust from napi-rs exhaust; nix bundle / AppImage yields an install-in-place folder.
round_trip_stages.inputs: The projected clean directory from S2.
mechanism: Nix deterministically compiles the projected crate against the x86_64-unknown-linux-musl target, forcing strict static linking so all dependencies are bundled into the binary and no hardcoded /nix/store RPATHs remain.
gitkb/meta scopes the compilation to the pure Rust directory, bypassing any napi-rs / wasm exhaust so the release artifact is untainted.
The output is packed into a portable install-in-place folder (nix bundle wrapped in AppImage or nix-user-chroot + buildEnv).

store_001_ownership.build_artifact: The musl binary and its closure are a derived projection: disposable and regenerable from the same PostgreSQL version; PostgreSQL remains the sole authority.

round_trip_stages.receipt: build_receipt — { nix_derivation_hash, target_triple: "x86_64-unknown-linux-musl", static: true, output_folder_path, binary_sha256, meta_include_scope }.
round_trip_stages.notes: Determinism of this stage is what the rebuild_proof asserts: same projected input -> byte-identical binary_sha256.

round_trip_stages.name: build-to-running-UDS (run + answer one request)
round_trip_stages.blueprint_pillars: Phase 4 Run + Execute — launch the portable folder; Yazelix initializes the Zellij + Nushell environment; LifeOS connects via Unix Domain Sockets.
round_trip_stages.inputs: The install-in-place folder / musl binary from S3.
mechanism: Launch the portable folder; Yazelix initializes the Zellij + Nushell background environment.
The binary binds a Unix Domain Socket and accepts one connection.
It receives the single lifeos.status request and returns the deterministic structured response, whose fields are read from canonical PostgreSQL identities / recorded stage receipts (not synthesized).

store_001_ownership.lifeos_control_surface: Per POSTGRES-007, every LifeOS UDS request/response maps to canonical PostgreSQL identities; redb-backed low-latency views may serve reads but never become a second authority; no hidden filesystem control-write path.

round_trip_stages.receipt: run_receipt — { socket_path, request: "lifeos.status", response_record, response_field_identity_map, pid, exit_status }.
round_trip_stages.notes: The behavior_proof consumes this receipt: the request was answered and every response field resolves to a canonical identity.

required_proofs.name: byte proof
required_proofs.claim: The projected files are byte-identical to the ingested source, and repeated projections are byte-identical to each other.
required_proofs.mechanism: Exact-byte materialization (ADOPT-001 capability) over the content-addressed BlobStore; envctl concatenation reconstructs raw_code blocks losslessly.
acceptance_criteria: For every slice member m: SHA-256(projected(m)) == SHA-256(source(m)).
For two independent projection runs r1, r2 of the same PostgreSQL version: SHA-256(projected_r1(m)) == SHA-256(projected_r2(m)) for every m (POSTGRES-008 byte-identical clause).
byte_len and file mode in projection_receipt match the source member exactly.

required_proofs.name: identity proof
required_proofs.claim: Every entity keeps exactly one stable identity across source -> database -> projection; no entity is assigned twice and none is silently reclassified.
required_proofs.mechanism: module_path metadata + SHA-256 content addressing form a bijection: source block <-> PostgreSQL row <-> projected byte-range. STORE-001 invariant: every migrated class keeps its SHA-256 identity.
acceptance_criteria: The identity map is a bijection over the slice: |source blocks| == |PostgreSQL rows| == |projected byte-ranges|, matched by (module_path, SHA-256).
Each slice member appears in exactly one STORE-001 state class (see state_class_participation) — no second owner, no dual-write.
No redb key for a slice block is treated as durable authority; redb entries are buffer/dedup only (STORE-001 single-durable-writer invariant).

required_proofs.name: behavior proof
required_proofs.claim: The statically built binary binds a UDS and returns the expected structured response to the one lifeos.status request.
required_proofs.mechanism: Run the install-in-place folder; send the single request; capture the response and resolve every field to a canonical PostgreSQL identity or recorded receipt.
acceptance_criteria: run_receipt shows the request sent and a response received with status == "ok".
Every response field (slice_id, projected_version_id, build_binary_sha256, answered_from) resolves through response_field_identity_map to a canonical PostgreSQL identity / stage receipt.
Process exits cleanly (exit_status == 0); no HTTP/REST path and no hidden filesystem control-write is used (POSTGRES-007).

required_proofs.name: provenance proof
required_proofs.claim: Every ingested block and every answered response field has a verifiable historical root; nothing is bluffed into system state.
required_proofs.mechanism: Source commit/PR lineage inherited via adopted_capabilities.source.csv (ADOPT-001); Cryptographic Witness Chains (SHAKE256) bind each block to its source vector; Causal Graph Verification (GNN) requires a causal edge; RuVector MinCut would sever a rootless node.
acceptance_criteria: Each PostgreSQL row for a slice block carries: source commit + PR, a causal dependency edge, and a SHAKE256 witness linking raw_code to its source vector.
Any block lacking a historical root in the knowledge graph is rejected (Causal Graph Verification) — the slice contains zero rootless blocks.
Each response field in run_receipt is traceable to a committed provenance record, not generated at request time.

required_proofs.name: rollback proof
required_proofs.claim: The entire proof is reversible using recorded receipts, leaving the original source and any installed profile unchanged.
required_proofs.mechanism: COW-branch discard semantics: the bounded namespace is a disposable branch; the projection tree and build folder are derived/disposable. Rollback deletes only derived state, driven by the receipts ledger.
acceptance_criteria: After rollback: the bounded PostgreSQL namespace/branch is dropped, the projected directory is removed, and the build folder is removed — all identified by receipt ids only.
Original source checksums (bounded_source_slice.members SHA-256) are unchanged; the installed profile is untouched (blocked_paths honored).
The rollback_receipt records zero destructive action against source or profile (PRESERVE-style completion evidence).
required_proofs.evidence_source: receipts_ledger + rollback (this artifact); matches the task rollback_plan verbatim.

required_proofs.name: rebuild proof
required_proofs.claim: The build is reproducible: re-projecting and re-building from the same PostgreSQL version yields a byte-identical binary, and re-embedding is reproducible.
required_proofs.mechanism: envctl projection is deterministic (module_path + content-addressed blocks); Nix musl static compilation is deterministic; the embedding model is pinned so re-embedding from the same BLAKE3-keyed text reproduces the same vector.
acceptance_criteria: Two independent runs of S2->S3 from the same source_version_id produce identical build_binary_sha256.
Projection is byte-identical across runs (shares evidence with P-BYTE second criterion).
Re-embedding the same block (same embedding_model_pin, same BLAKE3 key) reproduces the same semantic_embedding — no drift.
required_proofs.evidence_source: projection_receipt + build_receipt across two runs (S2, S3); embedding_model_pin in ingestion_receipt.

state_class_participation.state_class: durable relational macro-state (code AST/rows, module_path metadata, causal dependency edges)
state_class_participation.participation_in_slice: The three slice members' raw_code, module_path, and causal edges are committed here in S1 and are the source of truth for S2 projection.
state_class_participation.others_role: redb holds a transient forward buffer; envctl is the writer bridge (forward-only).

state_class_participation.participation_in_slice: semantic_embedding vectors for the slice blocks, produced by all-MiniLM-L6-v2 during the envctl bridge and committed to PostgreSQL.
state_class_participation.others_role: redb performs f32 distance/dedup math as a cache only; no SQL/secondary-index/native-vector logic in redb.

state_class_participation.state_class: transient high-frequency state (buffer entries, BLAKE3 dedup hashes, WAL during PostgreSQL latency)
state_class_participation.participation_in_slice: Slice blocks are buffered in redb and BLAKE3-hashed for dedup before envctl flushes them forward; on loss they are regenerable from source (transient by definition).
state_class_participation.others_role: PostgreSQL receives only the settled, significant commit; redb never becomes a second durable writer.

state_class_participation.participation_in_slice: EXCLUDED from the minimal slice — the one UDS request is answered from canonical PostgreSQL state, so no agent brain participates. Listed to prove it was decided, not forgotten.
state_class_participation.others_role: Non-authoritative for shared macro-state; witnessed by SHAKE256 chains rooted in PostgreSQL source vectors.

state_class_participation.state_class: derived projections (materialized source directory, musl build folder)
state_class_participation.canonical_owner: PostgreSQL (source of truth) projected by envctl
state_class_participation.participation_in_slice: The S2 projected directory and the S3 install-in-place build folder are derived and disposable; both are targets of the rollback proof.
state_class_participation.others_role: Filesystem projections are read-only outputs of envctl; regenerated deterministically from PostgreSQL rows.

state_class_participation.state_class: external-secret / provenance authority (API keys, source pins)
state_class_participation.participation_in_slice: EXCLUDED — secrets/** is never ingested; PostgreSQL stores provenance references (commit/PR lineage) but never raw secrets; redb never holds secrets.
state_class_participation.others_role: Provenance references in PostgreSQL support the provenance proof without holding the secrets themselves.

receipts_ledger.archive_root: One checksummed archive root holds all proof receipts so the round trip is auditable and reversible from a single place (PRESERVE-style completion evidence).

rollback.plan_verbatim: Delete the bounded database namespace and derived build projection using recorded receipts; original source and installed profile remain unchanged.
ordered_steps: Read ingestion_receipt.namespace_id/branch_id, projection_receipt.projected_tree_path, and build_receipt.output_folder_path from the receipts ledger.
Drop the bounded PostgreSQL namespace/branch (COW-branch discard) — removes S1 durable + semantic rows for the slice.
Remove the projected directory (S2) and the install-in-place build folder (S3) — derived/disposable state.
Verify original source checksums (bounded_source_slice.members SHA-256) are unchanged and the installed profile is untouched.
Write rollback_receipt recording zero destructive action against source or profile.
invariants_preserved: Original source bytes unchanged (byte identity verified post-rollback).
Installed profile untouched (profile installation is a blocked_path).
Only derived state (namespace, projection, build folder) is deleted.

store_001_alignment.single_durable_writer: PostgreSQL owns every durable macro-state class in the slice exactly once; redb is buffer/dedup only.
store_001_alignment.forward_only_sync: redb -> envctl(embed) -> PostgreSQL; no undirected redb<->PostgreSQL dual-write anywhere in the round trip.
store_001_alignment.no_sidecar: RuVector runs as a native PostgreSQL extension; the semantic vector column lives in PostgreSQL, not a separate vector database.
store_001_alignment.verified_cutover: This proof does not silently reclassify any redb-durable class; it exercises the intended path against a disposable bounded namespace, consistent with STORE-001's 'no unverified cutover' guardrail.
store_001_alignment.provenance_preserved: Every slice block keeps its SHA-256 identity and its source commit/PR lineage (adopted_capabilities.source.csv) plus a SHAKE256 witness.

open_dependencies: Live RuVector-in-PostgreSQL extension activation is not yet locally proven (STORE-001 semantic-class open dependency); until then the slice's embeddings are produced via the verified Bun-loaded ruvector N-API path and committed to PostgreSQL — the column owner stays PostgreSQL.

The live source-to-database-to-projection-to-build-to-run execution of this proof runs at the POSTGRES execution gate (POSTGRES-010 cutover lane), where a green blobstore parity run captures the durable-class cutover evidence. This definition supplies the acceptance criteria that execution must satisfy.
